input.context:
### data/app_pages/Education.md
## 3. Educational Goals and Current Status

### 3.1 Language Development

#### English Improvement (Priority)
- **Current Status:** Basic understanding, limited comprehension during serious conversations
- **Target:** Increase interaction time with Mother in English
- **Language Split Goals:**
  - **With Hirja:** 25-30% English, 70-75% Indonesian (daily or split by days/half-days)
  - **With Vanya:** 80% English, 20% Indonesian
  - **With Andrei:** 35% Russian, 65% English
- **Implementation:** If daily proportions are difficult, split by days or half-days and report which days used which language

#### Indonesian
- Primary language, good fluency for age

#### Russian
- Basic introduction, 35% target with Andrei

### 3.2 Numeracy Skills (High Priority)
**Participants:**
- Hirja (Indonesian)
- Vanya (Eglish)
- Andrei (Eglish)
- School (Eglish)
- **Current Knowledge:** 
  - Knows numbers and basic operations with numbers
  - Basic understanding of counting
  
- **Goals for This Month:**
  - Learn all numbers up to 100
  - Understand concept of scale: 100 → 1,000 → 1,000,000 (imagination/conceptual understanding)
  - Ability to name any number
  - Master basic operations: addition (+) and subtraction (-)
  
- **Educational Resources:**
  - Watch Numberblocks educational videos daily: 15 minutes to 1 hour
  - YouTube Channel: [Numberblocks Playlists](https://www.youtube.com/@Numberblocks/playlists)

### 3.3 Literacy Skills
**Participants:**
- Hirja (Eglish)
- Vanya (Eglish)
- Andrei (Eglish)
- School (Eglish)
- **Approach:** Begin after numbers are more established
- **Progression Strategy:**
  - When comfortable with numbers: Mix 5 days numbers + 2 days letters
  - When very good with numbers: 2 days numbers + 5 days letters
  - When no questions with numbers remain: Focus only on letters
  
- **Educational Resources:**
  - Watch Alphablocks educational videos: 15 minutes to 1 hour
  - YouTube Channel: [Alphablocks](https://www.youtube.com/@officialalphablocks)

### 3.4 Time Concepts
- **Approach:** After numbers are established
- **Teaching Schedule:**
  - First: Andrei teaches in English
  - Then: Vanya and Hirja teach in Indonesian

---

### data/app_pages/Dante Summary File.md
# Dante Summary File

**Last Updated:** January 10, 2026  
**Version:** 1.0  
**Document Purpose:** Comprehensive summary of Dante's background, current status, goals, strategies, and progress for AI group chat system and family coordination.

---

## 1. Basic Information

### 1.1 Personal Details
- **Full Name:** Ragnar Andante Vaulin (commonly known as Dante)
- **Date of Birth:** April 17, 2022
- **Age:** 3 years, 8 months, 24 days (approximately 4.73 years old, as of January 10, 2026)
- **Nationality:** Indonesian
- **Primary Language:** Indonesian (with basic English understanding)

### 1.2 Family Structure
- **Papa (Andrei):** Lives separately, sees Dante every Wednesday evening/night and full day on Sunday (mostly out of home)
  - Background: IT and life experience, generally widely smart, calm and reasonable
  - Languages: Russian (native), English (good), Indonesian (basic)
- **Mama (Vanya):** Lives separately, sees Dante almost daily but briefly
  - Background: Jakarta native, law background, described as very smart in narrow domains, powerful, impulsive, hard to deal with
  - Languages: Indonesian (native), English (fluent)
  - Current Status: Lives with boyfriend (adequate relationship), focuses primarily on work and her life, rarely takes Dante out of home
- **Primary Caregiver:** Hirja (Nanny)
  - Indonesian (native), English -Middle
  - Very kind and simple person, love him as his own
  - Spends most time with Dante
  - Sleep with him in one room
  - Studied Germany
  - Reading Books
  - Muslim
  - 28 years (double check)
  - Like nature and travel
- **Extended Family:** 
  - Grandparents and relatives mostly in Belarus or Jakarta
  - Generally good roots but with family-specific characteristics

### 1.3 Living Situation
- Papa and Mama maintain generally good connection despite living separately
- Dante spends majority of time with Nanny (Hirja)
- Primary residence structure supports shared custody arrangement
- He has his own cat and live with a old dog and cat as well. 

---

## 2. Psychological Portrait and Behavioral Type

### 2.1 General Character
- **Overall Nature:** Generally kind and nice child, very active, with own attitude, very handsome, social, 
- **Personality Traits:** 
  - Shows leadership tendencies (thinks he is "hulk" and may display related assertive behaviors)
  - Capable of kindness and good behavior
- **Potential Conditions:** 
  - Probable ADHD (difficulty focusing on activities for more than 5 minutes, even during long movies)
  - Attention span limitations observed




## 12. Important Reminders for All Caregivers



### 12.4 Communication Rules
- **Always reference Papa's authority** when saying no or setting boundaries
- **Never show disrespect** toward Andrei in Dante's presence
- **Report immediately** any concerning behaviors, health issues, or accidents
- **Ask questions** if anything is unclear rather than guessing
- **Update immediately** when plans change or issues arise
- **Use group chat** for all communication related to Dante

---

**End of Dante Summary File**


### data/app_pages/Behavioral Data.md
### 2.2 Behavioral Patterns
- **Positive Behaviors:**
  - Generally kind disposition
  - Shows interest in learning when engaged properly
  - Can be cooperative when properly motivated
  
- **Challenging Behaviors:**
  - Occasional episodes of "bad behavior"
  - Tendency toward physical conflicts when displeased
  - Impulsive and hysterical outbursts seemingly "from nothing"
  - Difficulty listening, especially during emotional states
  - Can "shut down" and become non-responsive when confronted seriously
  
- **Behavioral Triggers:**
  - Disagreement or being told "no"
  - Situations that don't align with his wants
  - Transition periods or schedule changes
  - When feeling overwhelmed or overstimulated

### 2.3 Authority and Response Patterns
- **Authority Hierarchy Observed:**
  - Papa (Andrei) — highest authority, though authority is decreasing
  - When Papa speaks seriously, Dante may:
    - Request to go to Mama
    - Shut down and become non-responsive
  - This suggests authority boundaries need reinforcement

### 2.4 Social Interactions
- Beginning stages of parallel play with other children
- Sharing behaviors still developing
- Friendship formation observed but still early stages

---


## 4. Current Problems and Challenges

### 4.1 Behavioral Challenges
1. **Impulsive Outbursts:**
   - Can become very impulsive and hysterical
   - Outbursts sometimes occur "from nothing"
   - Difficulty managing emotions when frustrated

2. **Listening and Compliance:**
   - Often does not listen, especially during emotional states
   - Declining respect for Papa's authority
   - When confronted seriously, may shut down or request to leave situation

3. **Conflict Resolution:**
   - Tendency to fight when something doesn't go his way
   - Physical reactions to disagreements
   - Needs development in verbal conflict resolution

4. **Attention and Focus:**
   - Cannot focus on activities for more than 5 minutes
   - Difficulty watching long movies or extended activities
   - Probable ADHD characteristics requiring accommodation

### 4.2 Educational Challenges
1. **Language Comprehension:**
   - Limited English understanding during serious conversations
   - Difficulty following complex instructions in English
   - Need for gradual language transition

2. **Engagement:**
   - Resistance to educational content (e.g., Numberblocks, Alphablocks)
   - Preference for entertainment over education
   - Need for creative motivation strategies

### 4.3 Family Dynamics Challenges
1. **Authority Structure:**
   - Decreasing respect for Papa's authority
   - Need for consistent reinforcement across all caregivers
   - Vanya must always reference Andrei respectfully: "Papa said no because..." to model respect

2. **Mama's Involvement:**
   - Limited time and engagement from Vanya
   - Need for increased structured interaction
   - Important for bonding and authority modeling

---

## 5. Current Strategy and Approach

### 5.1 Behavioral Management Strategy

#### Discipline Approach
- **Escalation Process (Recommended):**
  1. Normal explanation and request (10-15% intensity)
  2. Not soft, but appropriate for hard situations
  3. After several normal attempts, escalation acceptable
  4. After escalation, MUST make peace:
     - Hug and reconnect
     - Name the situation and what happened
     - Acknowledge both his emotions and caregiver's emotions
     - Reassure that love remains, everything will be okay
     - Reinforce that he is a kind, good boy
  
- **Alternative Approach (Not Recommended):**
  - Occasional firm voice acceptable if self-control difficult
  - However, long-term risks include:
    - Child may learn to hate screamers (as seen in Papa's experience)
    - Potential relationship damage
  - **Note:** This approach is "workable" but not ideal

#### Authority Reinforcement
- **Critical Rule:** Vanya must NEVER behave disrespectfully toward Andrei in Dante's presence
- Always reference Papa's decisions: "Papa said no because..." or "Papa said this because..."
- Model respect so Dante learns to respect authority
- Consistent messaging across all caregivers

#### Daily Reflection Questions
Every day, ask Dante:
- What did you do that was good today?
- Whom did you help today?
- To whom were you kind today?
- Who was kind to you today?
- What new thing did you learn or find out today?
- How many times did you try before something worked (or didn't work)?

output.response:
Based on Dante's profile, here are some practical steps to help him engage with learning numbers:

1.  **Address Potential ADHD:** Given the possibility of ADHD and his short attention span, consider consulting with a specialist for assessment and tailored strategies.
2.  **Incorporate Interests:**
    *   **Gamification:** Turn learning numbers into a game. Use toys, blocks, or even his "Hulk" persona to count and perform basic operations.
    *   **Relate to Interests:** Connect numbers to his interests like nature or travel (mentioned in Hirja's profile). For example, count the number of trees in a park or talk about distances between places.
3.  **Short, Engaging Sessions:**
    *   **Limit Time:** Keep numeracy sessions short (5-15 minutes) to match his attention span.
    *   **Variety:** Switch activities frequently to maintain engagement.
4.  **Utilize Educational Resources:**
    *   **Numberblocks:** Continue using Numberblocks videos but break them into smaller segments. Make it interactive by pausing and asking questions.
5.  **Positive Reinforcement:**
    *   **Reward System:** Use a reward system for completing numeracy tasks. This could be verbal praise, stickers, or small, non-material rewards.
    *   **Focus on Progress:** Acknowledge and praise his efforts and improvements, not just the final result.
6.  **Language Considerations:**
    *   **English and Indonesian:** Use a mix of English and Indonesian when teaching numbers, especially with Hirja. Gradually increase the English component as his comprehension improves.
7.  **Incorporate Movement:**
    *   **Active Learning:** Combine learning with physical activity. For example, count steps while walking or jumping.
8.  **Consistent Approach Among Caregivers:**
    *   **Unified Strategy:** Ensure all caregivers (Hirja, Vanya, and Andrei) use a consistent approach and reinforce the same concepts.
9.  **Address Behavioral Triggers:**
    *   **Avoid Overwhelm:** Be mindful of situations that trigger negative behaviors. Break down tasks into smaller, manageable steps to prevent him from feeling overwhelmed.
    *   **Positive Communication:** Use positive language and avoid direct confrontation. Frame learning as a fun activity rather than a chore.
10. **Authority and Respect:**
    *   **Papa's Authority:** Reinforce Andrei's authority by referencing his decisions in a respectful manner. This helps establish boundaries and encourages compliance.
11. **Daily Reflection:**
    *   **Adapt Questions:** Modify the daily reflection questions to include numeracy-related topics. For example, "How many things did you count today?"

It would be beneficial to have more information on Dante's specific interests and preferred learning methods to tailor the approach further. Additionally, consulting with an educational psychologist or specialist could provide more targeted strategies for addressing his potential ADHD and learning challenges.

output.response:
Based on Dante's preference for rice and chicken and his general behavioral patterns, here are some strategies to encourage him to eat vegetables:

1.  **Incorporate Vegetables into Preferred Foods:**
    *   Puree vegetables and mix them into sauces for the rice or chicken. This can mask the taste and texture of the vegetables.
    *   Finely chop vegetables and add them to chicken dishes like stir-fries or casseroles.

2.  **Make Vegetables Fun and Appealing:**
    *   Cut vegetables into fun shapes using cookie cutters.
    *   Arrange vegetables on a plate in a creative way, like making a face or a picture.
    *   Serve vegetables with a dip that Dante enjoys, such as hummus or a yogurt-based dip.

3.  **Introduce Vegetables Gradually:**
    *   Offer small portions of vegetables alongside his preferred foods.
    *   Introduce one new vegetable at a time to see which ones he might be more receptive to.

4.  **Positive Reinforcement:**
    *   Praise and reward Dante for trying vegetables, even if he only takes a small bite. Avoid using food as a reward (e.g., "If you eat your vegetables, you can have dessert"). Instead, offer non-food rewards like stickers or extra playtime.

5.  **Model Good Eating Habits:**
    *   Ensure that all caregivers (Mama, Papa, and Hirja) eat vegetables and show enjoyment while doing so. Dante is more likely to try vegetables if he sees others eating them.

6.  **Involve Dante in Meal Preparation:**
    *   Let Dante help wash, chop (with supervision), or prepare vegetables. He may be more willing to try something he helped create.

7.  **Consistent Messaging and Authority:**
    *   Since Dante responds to authority, ensure all caregivers are consistent in encouraging him to eat vegetables. Referencing Papa's authority might be helpful: "Papa wants you to try some vegetables because they are good for you."

8.  **Address Potential ADHD Characteristics:**
    *   Given Dante's potential ADHD, keep mealtimes structured and minimize distractions. Offer vegetables at the beginning of the meal when he is less likely to be overwhelmed.

9. **Escalation Process:**
*   Follow the recommended escalation process for discipline. Start with a normal explanation and request, and escalate if necessary. Always make peace afterward with a hug and reassurance.

To provide more tailored advice, it would be helpful to know:

*   **What vegetables have already been tried and what was Dante's reaction to them?**
*   **What are the typical mealtime dynamics and environment?**
*   **What are some of Dante's favorite characters or stories?** (These could be used to create themed meals or make the vegetables more appealing.)

### data/app_pages/Schedule.md
## 8. Schedule Overview

### 8.1 Monday - Friday Structure
- **07:30** — Wake up and bath routine
- **08:00** — Breakfast
- **08:30** — Go to school
- **13:30** — Lunch (food from home)
- **14:00 - 17:00/19:00** — After-school activities with Hirja (5 days) or Vanya (at least once per month, preferably 2 times)
  - Keep activities varied daily
  - Plan next day one day before and mention in report
  - Add new options as ideas arise
  - At least once per month: Vanya takes him somewhere just the two of them for 5 hours
  - Special events: Prison visits, charity visits, seeing how people in difficult situations live (organized by Vanya and Hirja, conversations prepared by Andrei)
- **19:00 - 21:00/22:00** — Home time
  - 4 days with Vanya (Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday) — at least 2 days with active playing and full contact (not just watching him or watching movies)
  - 1 day with Andrei (Wednesday)
  - Clean cat toilet (responsibility)

### 8.2 Saturday Structure (Hirja)
- **07:30** — Wake up and bath routine (Flexible)
- **08:00** — Breakfast (Flexible)
- **08:30 - 19:00** — Go somewhere (Budget: 250,000 IDR)
  - Prepare list of places (Vanya and Andrei may add)
  - If new places discovered, inform everyone and add to list
- **19:00 - 21:00/22:00** — Home
  - Watch long-term cartoons/movies in English (1.5 hours) or TV series
    - Everyone to make list with at least 10 options with links
    - If problems finding content online, notify Andrei
    - Try to excite him about content even if initial resistance
    - Watch WITH him
    - Report any problems
    - It's okay to rewatch favorites, but show something new each week
  - Sometimes: Sleepover with Andrei if available

### 8.3 Sunday Structure (Andrei)
- **07:30** — Wake up and bath routine (Flexible)
- **08:00** — Breakfast (Flexible)
- **10:00 - 22:00** — Go somewhere, stay at home if not weather, or combination (Flexible)
  - Once per month: Vanya joins for at least 5 hours
    - Vanya should notify 1 day before (preferably earlier)
    - Rotate who chooses activity: Vanya chooses one day, Andrei next, etc.
  - Sometimes: Can stay sleepover with Andrei until school Monday

---

output.response: On Sundays, the schedule includes waking up and bath routine around 7:30 (flexible), breakfast around 8:00 (flexible), and going somewhere or staying at home (flexible) from 10:00 to 22:00. Once a month, Vanya joins for at least 5 hours, and they rotate who chooses the activity. Sometimes, there can be a sleepover with Andrei until school on Monday.
